cards.title: Supporting Sensitive Teens Through Social Media Pressure
cards.body: Social media and peer comparison can test a teen's self-worth, especially for sensitive children. As they mature, their social world naturally expands, offering more opportunities to find like-minded peers. Open communication and perspective-taking help build resilience.

cards.title: Music Education Builds Critical Life Skills in Children
cards.body: Music education develops executive function skills like self-control and planning that lead to greater life success. Learning music helps children behave thoughtfully and make smart decisions. These cognitive benefits extend far beyond musical ability.

cards.title: Twins Need Help Developing Individual Identities
cards.body: Twin closeness, while special, can hinder each child's development of their own distinctive identity. Parents should encourage twins to pursue separate interests and friendships. Supporting individuality helps twins thrive as separate people.

cards.title: What Parents Should Know About Teen Trouble Signs
cards.body: Recent legal cases show parents must stay informed about their teens' activities and struggles. Being aware and prepared helps prevent serious behavioral issues before they escalate. Open dialogue and engagement are essential parenting tools.

cards.title: Teaching Kids About Money and Self-Worth
cards.body: Before children understand finances, they need to know they matter and their contributions count. Early lessons about contribution build healthy money attitudes later. Making kids feel valued is the foundation of financial literacy.

cards.title: MenB Vaccine Recommended for UK Teenagers Aged 15
cards.body: UK health experts recommend introducing MenB vaccines for teenagers from age 15 following a meningitis B outbreak. The outbreak in Kent resulted in two deaths, prompting the safety review. Vaccination protects adolescents during higher-risk years.
